reaches.name: Alameda Creek mainstem and tributaries downstream of reservoirs (excl. Arroyo Del Valle segment)
reaches.fromDesc: Alameda Creek mainstem and all tributaries downstream of San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs
reaches.toDesc: Confluence with San Francisco Bay near Niles Canyon / Fremont, excluding the Arroyo Del Valle segment between Bernal Ave. and Vineyard Ave.

reaches.name: Arroyo Del Valle (Bernal Ave. to Thiessen St./Vineyard Ave.)
reaches.toDesc: Thiessen St. intersection with Vineyard Ave., Pleasanton

reaches.name: Alameda Creek tributaries upstream of San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs
reaches.fromDesc: Headwater tributaries above San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs
reaches.toDesc: Upper watershed streams in the Diablo Range, Alameda and Santa Clara counties

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 1654946, "Alameda Creek", Stream, CA, Alameda/Santa Clara Cos.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":1654946,"gaz_name":"Alameda Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","state_alpha":"CA"} — geometry multipoint; first point converts to lon -122.1469, lat 37.5944, near the creek's mouth at San Francisco Bay, correcting the file's prior coordinate (-121.8992, 37.5952) which sat ~22km east along the mainstem near Niles Canyon/Sunol and did not match any GNIS-derived point.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(2) — Alameda Creek and tributaries (Alameda and Santa Clara Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (2) Alameda Creek and tributaries (Alameda and Santa Clara Cos.). (A) Alameda Creek mainstem and all tributaries downstream of San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs except for Arroyo Del Valle between Bernal Ave. and the Thiessen St. intersection with Vineyard Ave. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
How the SFPUC Contributes to Making Alameda Creek a Haven for Fish
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Alameda Creek mainstem and all tributaries downstream of San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs are closed to all fishing year-round, except the Arroyo Del Valle segment between Bernal Ave. and the Thiessen St./Vineyard Ave. intersection, which is separately regulated.

regulations.humanSummary: Alameda Creek mainstem and tributaries downstream of the major reservoirs are closed to all fishing year-round (protects listed steelhead)
regulations.verbatimText: Alameda Creek mainstem and all tributaries downstream of San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs except for Arroyo Del Valle between Bernal Ave. and the Thiessen St. intersection with Vineyard Ave. Closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Arroyo Del Valle segment between Bernal Ave. and Vineyard Ave. is open to fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: Arroyo Del Valle between Bernal Ave. and the Thiessen St. intersection with Vineyard Ave. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used on this Arroyo Del Valle segment
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Zero trout may be retained; all trout caught must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout bag limit — catch and release only
Tributaries upstream of San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30
regulations.verbatimText: Alameda Creek tributaries upstream of San Antonio, Calaveras, and Del Valle Reservoirs. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used on upstream tributaries
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Zero trout may be retained; all trout caught must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout bag limit on upstream tributaries — catch and release only
San Antonio and Calaveras Reservoirs open to fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: San Antonio and Calaveras Reservoirs. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
